e Lagna.
From the time of sunrise (local mean time) to the given
local mean time of birth, note the ghatikas and the Vighatikas
that have elapsed.
Each ghati is equal to twenty four minutes, and each
Vighati has twenty four seconds. An hour is equal to two and
a half Ghati. A ghati has sixty Vighatis.
Convert the duration into Vighatis. Multiply this by four
and divide it by nine. The remainder counted from Asvini,
Magha, and Mula must coincide with the star at the time of

Traditional Methods 13
birth. If it does not agree, the birth time has to be adjusted
accordingly.
Suppose the birth was at ten Ghatis and fifty five
Vighatis. This gives 655 Vighatis. This multiplied by four is
2620 and divided by nine the remainder is one. It is Asvini,
Magha or Mula. But if the birth star is Sravana, the remain
der should be four. We have then to add three Vighatis or
deduct six Vighatis. The birth then must be at gh 10.58 or at
gh 10.49.
The second method has to be applied to determine when
